Not available in the selected region

Virtual Machine Scale Sets

Manage and scale up to thousands of Linux and Windows virtual machines

Virtual Machine scale sets make it easy to build highly scalable applications by allowing you to effortlessly deploy and manage a set of VMs as a group. Built on the Azure Resource Manager deployment model, VM scale sets are fully integrated with Azure load balancing and autoscale and support Windows, Linux, custom images, and extensions. VM scale sets offer two modes of operation:

1.Scale Set VMs – this provides easy creation and management of identical VMs

2.Single VMs – this provide easy way to add your VMs to VMSS during the creation process (e.g., to obtain high availability).

Virtual Machine scale sets are currently available on all Azure Windows or Linux VM sizes. You are only charged for the Azure VMs you deploy, as well as any additional underlying infrastructure resources consumed such as storage and networking. There are no incremental charges for the Virtual Machine scale sets service itself. For the pricing details of virtual machine, please visit the Virtual Machines Pricing Page .

Choose from the following payment options for Azure Virtual Machines scale sets.

1.Pay-in-advance is the price which is available for Pay-in-advance customers, who purchased or plan to purchase Microsoft Azure services operated by 21Vianet online through Azure China Website (under 21Vianet Online Services Standard Agreement, also called OSSA)

2.CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Services Premium Agreement (also called OSPA). You may contact your account manager or Partner if you’d like purchase CPP
CPP saving percentage=(Pay-in-advance price by month/(1+6%)-CPP price )/ (Pay-in-advance price by month/(1+6%)), take standard A series A1(Linux) for instance, saving percentage=（￥215.76/(1+6%）-￥64.17)/ (￥215.76/(1+6%）)=68%.
Azure Compute Pre-Purchase is the most cost-effective way for OSPA Azure customers to get discount on compute resources (i.e. VM in OSPA Azure).

General purpose: balancing of CPU to memory ratio. Suitable for testing and development of small- and medium-sized databases and low- and medium-traffic Web servers.

A0-4 – Basic

A Basic is an economical option for development workloads, test servers, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases.

The A Series standard tier supports applications requiring load balancing and autoscaling. These Virtual Machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repository, low-traffic websites and Web Apps, microservices, early product trials and small databases.

B Series

B series are economical virtual machines that provide a low-cost option for workloads which typically run at a low to moderate baseline CPU performance, but sometimes need to burst to significantly higher CPU performance when the demand rises. These workloads don’t require the use of the full CPU all the time, but occasionally will need to burst to finish some tasks more quickly. Many applications such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, small databases, micro services, servers for proof-of-concepts, build servers, and code repositories fit into this model.

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

Av2 Standard

Av2 Standard is the latest generation of A-series virtual machines with similar CPU performance and faster disk. These virtual machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases. Like the prior A Standard generation, Av2 virtual machines will include load balancing and auto-scaling at no additional charge.

Dv3

The Dv3-series virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors in a hyper-threaded configuration. The Dv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk well suited for most production workloads.

Dsv3

The Dsv3-series support premium storage and run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0 and feature Intel® Hyper-Threading Technology. The Dsv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and temporary storage well suited for most production workloads.

The VM pricing and billing meters for Dsv3-series are the same as Dv3-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2 – D64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The D v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The D v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Ds v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

The Ds v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The Ds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2d – D64d v4 (latest generation)

The Dd v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would benefit from these new VM series.

The Dd v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, select Dds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

D2ds – D64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Dds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

The Dds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

The D1-4/DS 1-4 instances involve provision of powerful CPU, memory and local disk combinations for most production applications.

For persistent storage, please use variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

The D1-5 v2 sizes offer a balanced combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk for most production workloads.

Computing optimization: high CPU to memory. Suitable for medium-traffic Web server, network device, batch processing and App Services

Fs v2 Series new

The Fsv2-series virtual machines provide 2-GiB of RAM and 8 GB of local temporary storage (SSD) per vCPU(s) and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The Fsv2-series VMs are hyper-threaded and based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), or the Intel Xeon® Platinum 8168 (Skylake) processor. These virtual machines are ideal for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming. The Fsv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

F Series

The F-series virtual machines feature 2-GiB RAM and 16 GiB of local SSD temporary storage per CPU core and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The F-series sizes are based Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processor. These virtual machines are suitable for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSDs disks, use the Fs-series VMs. The pricing and billing meters for Fs sizes are the same as F-series. See pricing for disks .

The Ev3-series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake) or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell) processor with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0. The Ev3-series VMs are hyper-threaded and ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSD disks, choose the Esv3-series virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2 – E64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The E v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Es v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

The Es v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2d – E64d v4 (latest generation)

The Ed v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB.) These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications and applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would also benefit from these new VM series.

You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD disk storage, select Eds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

E2ds – E64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Eds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D11-14/DS11-14

The D11-14/DS11-14 instances are suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications.

For persistent storage, please use the variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

D11-15 v2

D11-15 v2 virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The D11-15 v2 VMs are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The D15i v2 instance is isolated to hardware and dedicated to a single customer.

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

DS 11-15 v2

D11-15S v2 virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The DSv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. The pricing and billing meters for DSv2 sizes are the same as Dv2-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

M-series

M-series virtual machines are hyper-threaded and based on either the Intel® Xeon® E7-8890 v3 2.5GHz (Haswell) or the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8280M (Cascade Lake). The M-series provides up to 128 cores and 4.0 TiB of memory, providing unparalleled computational performance to support large in-memory workloads.

All the M-series virtual machine sizes will have the ability to use both standard and premium persistent disks. The pricing and billing meters for Ms sizes are the same as M-series.

M-series VMs are certified by SAP for SAP HANA OLTP and OLAP workloads. For more information on running SAP HANA on Azure, please refer to the SAP solutions .

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

Constrained vCPUs capable

These new VM sizes are optimized for database workloads like SQL Server that often require high memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth, but not a high CPU core count. They constrain the VM vCPU(s) count to reduce the cost of software licensing, all while maintaining the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth. The licensing charged for SQL Server will be constrained to the active vCPU(s) count while the compute cost, which includes OS licensing, remains the same one as the original size based on “underlying vCPU(s)”. These new VM sizes allow customer workloads to use the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth while optimizing their software licensing cost.

GPU：Specialized virtual machines targeted for heavy graphic rendering and video editing available with single or multiple GPUs.

NCsv3 series new

NCsv3 series virtual machines is a new addition to the GPU product family offering the next generation of our popular NC-series machines, powered by NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs. Customers can take advantage of these updated GPUs for traditional HPC workloads that will benefit from a performance boost, powering scenarios like reservoir modeling, DNA sequencing, protein analysis, Monte Carlo simulations, and others. These new GPUs can provide 1.5 times the computational performance of the current NCsv2-series. And like our other NC series, we offer a configuration, NC24rs v3, with InfiniBand networking for workloads that require fast interconnect, like Oil and Gas, Automotive, and Genomics to also accelerate scale out capability as well as improved single instance performance.

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

*Please notice: The following service prices are Tax-Inclusive except CPP (Compute Pre-Purchase). CPP (Compute Pre-Purchase) Prices are Tax-Exclusion in monthly base.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

General purpose: balancing of CPU to memory ratio. Suitable for testing and development of small- and medium-sized databases and low- and medium-traffic Web servers.

A0-4 – Basic

A Basic is an economical option for development workloads, test servers, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases.

A Series Standard

The A Series standard tier supports applications requiring load balancing and autoscaling. These Virtual Machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repository, low-traffic websites and Web Apps, microservices, early product trials and small databases.

B Series

B series are economical virtual machines that provide a low-cost option for workloads which typically run at a low to moderate baseline CPU performance, but sometimes need to burst to significantly higher CPU performance when the demand rises. These workloads don’t require the use of the full CPU all the time, but occasionally will need to burst to finish some tasks more quickly. Many applications such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, small databases, micro services, servers for proof-of-concepts, build servers, and code repositories fit into this model.

Av2 Standard

Av2 Standard is the latest generation of A-series virtual machines with similar CPU performance and faster disk. These virtual machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases. Like the prior A Standard generation, Av2 virtual machines will include load balancing and auto-scaling at no additional charge.

Dv3

The Dv3-series virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors in a hyper-threaded configuration. The Dv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk well suited for most production workloads.

Dsv3

The Dsv3-series support premium storage and run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0 and feature Intel® Hyper-Threading Technology. The Dsv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and temporary storage well suited for most production workloads.

The VM pricing and billing meters for Dsv3-series are the same as Dv3-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2 – D64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The D v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The D v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Ds v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2s – D64s v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The Ds v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The Ds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2d – D64d v4 (latest generation)

The Dd v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would benefit from these new VM series.

The Dd v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, select Dds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

D2ds – D64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Dds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

The Dds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

For persistent storage, please use variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

The D1-5 v2 sizes offer a balanced combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk for most production workloads.

Computing optimization: high CPU to memory. Suitable for medium-traffic Web server, network device, batch processing and App Services

Fs v2 Series new

The Fsv2-series virtual machines provide 2-GiB of RAM and 8 GB of local temporary storage (SSD) per vCPU(s) and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The Fsv2-series VMs are hyper-threaded and based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), or the Intel Xeon® Platinum 8168 (Skylake) processor. These virtual machines are ideal for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming. The Fsv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

F Series

The F-series virtual machines feature 2-GiB RAM and 16 GiB of local SSD temporary storage per CPU core and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The F-series sizes are based Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processor. These virtual machines are suitable for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSDs disks, use the Fs-series VMs. The pricing and billing meters for Fs sizes are the same as F-series. See pricing for disks .

E2 – E64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The E v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Es v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2d – E64d v4 (latest generation)

The Ed v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB.) These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications and applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would also benefit from these new VM series.

You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD disk storage, select Eds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

E2ds – E64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Eds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D11-14/DS11-14

The D11-14/DS11-14 instances are suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications.

For persistent storage, please use variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

D11-15 v2

D11-15 v2 virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The D11-15 v2 VMs are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The D15i v2 instance is isolated to hardware and dedicated to a single customer.

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

DS 11-15 v2

D11-15S v2 virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The DSv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. The pricing and billing meters for DSv2 sizes are the same as Dv2-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

M-series

M-series virtual machines are hyper-threaded and based on either the Intel® Xeon® E7-8890 v3 2.5GHz (Haswell) or the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8280M (Cascade Lake). The M-series provides up to 128 cores and 4.0 TiB of memory, providing unparalleled computational performance to support large in-memory workloads.

All the M-series virtual machine sizes will have the ability to use both standard and premium persistent disks. The pricing and billing meters for Ms sizes are the same as M-series.

M-series VMs are certified by SAP for SAP HANA OLTP and OLAP workloads. For more information on running SAP HANA on Azure, please refer to the SAP solutions .

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

Constrained vCPUs capable

These new VM sizes are optimized for database workloads like SQL Server that often require high memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth, but not a high CPU core count. They constrain the VM vCPU(s) count to reduce the cost of software licensing, all while maintaining the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth. The licensing charged for SQL Server will be constrained to the active vCPU(s) count while the compute cost, which includes OS licensing, remains the same one as the original size based on “underlying vCPU(s)”. These new VM sizes allow customer workloads to use the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth while optimizing their software licensing cost.

GPU：Specialized virtual machines targeted for heavy graphic rendering and video editing available with single or multiple GPUs.

NCsv3 series new

NCsv3 series virtual machines is a new addition to the GPU product family offering the next generation of our popular NC-series machines, powered by NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs. Customers can take advantage of these updated GPUs for traditional HPC workloads that will benefit from a performance boost, powering scenarios like reservoir modeling, DNA sequencing, protein analysis, Monte Carlo simulations, and others. These new GPUs can provide 1.5 times the computational performance of the current NCsv2-series. And like our other NC series, we offer a configuration, NC24rs v3, with InfiniBand networking for workloads that require fast interconnect, like Oil and Gas, Automotive, and Genomics to also accelerate scale out capability as well as improved single instance performance.

China CPP(Compute Pre-Purchase) is the special Service SKU which is available for customers who signed or plan to sign 21Vianet Online Service Premium Agreement (also called OSPA).

*Please notice: The following service prices are Tax-Inclusive except CPP (Compute Pre-Purchase). CPP (Compute Pre-Purchase) Prices are Tax-Exclusion in monthly base.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

The following prices include the pricing of both Windows Virtual Machine compute and SQL Server license.

General purpose: balancing of CPU to memory ratio. Suitable for testing and development of small- and medium-sized databases and low- and medium-traffic Web servers.

A0-4 – Basic

A Basic is an economical option for development workloads, test servers, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases.

B series

B series are economical virtual machines that provide a low-cost option for workloads that typically run at a low to moderate baseline CPU performance, but sometimes need to burst to significantly higher CPU performance when the demand rises. These workloads don’t require the use of the full CPU all the time, but occasionally will need to burst to finish some tasks more quickly. Many applications such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, small databases, micro services, servers for proof-of-concepts, build servers, and code repositories fit into this model.

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Av2 Standard

Av2 Standard is the latest generation of A-series virtual machines with similar CPU performance and faster disk. These virtual machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases. Like the prior A Standard generation, Av2 virtual machines will include load balancing and auto-scaling at no additional charge.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Dv3

The Dv3-series virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors in a hyper-threaded configuration. The Dv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk well suited for most production workloads.

Dsv3

The Dsv3-series support premium storage and run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0 and feature Intel® Hyper-Threading Technology. The Dsv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and temporary storage well suited for most production workloads.

The VM pricing and billing meters for Dsv3-series are the same as Dv3-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

D2 – D64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The D v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The D v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Ds v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2s – D64s v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The Ds v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The Ds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2d – D64d v4 (latest generation)

The Dd v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would benefit from these new VM series.

The Dd v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, select Dds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

D2ds – D64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Dds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

The Dds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

Computing optimization: high CPU to memory. Suitable for medium-traffic Web server, network device, batch processing and App Services

Fsv2-series

The Fsv2-series virtual machines provide 2-GiB of RAM and 8 GB of local temporary storage (SSD) per vCPU(s) and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The Fsv2-series VMs are hyper-threaded and based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), or the Intel Xeon® Platinum 8168 (Skylake) processor. These virtual machines are ideal for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming. The Fsv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

*The following prices include the pricing of both Windows Virtual Machine and SQL Server.

F Series

The F-series virtual machines feature 2-GiB RAM and 16 GiB of local SSD temporary storage per CPU core and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The F-series sizes are based Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processor. These virtual machines are suitable for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSDs disks, use the Fs-series VMs. The pricing and billing meters for Fs sizes are the same as F-series. See pricing for disks .

Memory prioritization: high memory to core ratio. Suitable for relational database servers, medium- and large-scale caches and memory analysis.

Ev3

The Ev3-series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake) or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell) processor with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0. The Ev3-series VMs are hyper-threaded and ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSD disks, choose the Esv3-series virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

ESv3

The Esv3-series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake) or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell) processor with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0 and support Premium SSDs disk storage. The Ev3-series sizes are hyper-threaded and ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSDs disks, use the Esv3 VM series. The pricing and billing meters for Esv3 sizes are the same as Ev3-series. See pricing for disks .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

E2 – E64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The E v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Es v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2s – E64s v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The Es v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2d – E64d v4 (latest generation)

The Ed v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB.) These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications and applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would also benefit from these new VM series.

You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD disk storage, select Eds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

E2ds – E64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Eds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D11-15 v2

D11-15 v2 virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The D11-15 v2 VMs are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The D15i v2 instance is isolated to hardware and dedicated to a single customer.

DS 11-15 v2

D11-15S v2 virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The DSv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. The pricing and billing meters for DSv2 sizes are the same as Dv2-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

*The following prices include the pricing of both Windows Virtual Machine and SQL Server.

M-series

M-series virtual machines are hyper-threaded and based on either the Intel® Xeon® E7-8890 v3 2.5GHz (Haswell) or the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8280M (Cascade Lake). The M-series provides up to 128 cores and 4.0 TiB of memory, providing unparalleled computational performance to support large in-memory workloads.

All the M-series virtual machine sizes will have the ability to use both standard and premium persistent disks. The pricing and billing meters for Ms sizes are the same as M-series.

M-series VMs are certified by SAP for SAP HANA OLTP and OLAP workloads. For more information on running SAP HANA on Azure, please refer to the SAP solutions .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Constrained vCPUs capable

These new VM sizes are optimized for database workloads like SQL Server that often require high memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth, but not a high CPU core count. They constrain the VM vCPU(s) count to reduce the cost of software licensing, all while maintaining the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth. The licensing charged for SQL Server will be constrained to the active vCPU(s) count while the compute cost, which includes OS licensing, remains the same one as the original size based on “underlying vCPU(s)”. These new VM sizes allow customer workloads to use the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth while optimizing their software licensing cost.

GPU：Specialized virtual machines targeted for heavy graphic rendering and video editing available with single or multiple GPUs.

NCsv3 series new

NCsv3 series virtual machines is a new addition to the GPU product family offering the next generation of our popular NC-series machines, powered by NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs. Customers can take advantage of these updated GPUs for traditional HPC workloads that will benefit from a performance boost, powering scenarios like reservoir modeling, DNA sequencing, protein analysis, Monte Carlo simulations, and others. These new GPUs can provide 1.5 times the computational performance of the current NCsv2-series. And like our other NC series, we offer a configuration, NC24rs v3, with InfiniBand networking for workloads that require fast interconnect, like Oil and Gas, Automotive, and Genomics to also accelerate scale out capability as well as improved single instance performance.

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

  • Computing Prioritization
  • Memory Prioritization

The following prices include the pricing of both Linux Virtual Machine compute and SQL Server license.

General purpose: balancing of CPU to memory ratio. Suitable for testing and development of small- and medium-sized databases and low- and medium-traffic Web servers.

A0-4 – Basic

A Basic is an economical option for development workloads, test servers, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases.

A Series Standard

The A Series standard tier supports applications requiring load balancing and autoscaling. These Virtual Machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repository, low-traffic websites and Web Apps, microservices, early product trials and small databases.

B series

B series are economical virtual machines that provide a low-cost option for workloads that typically run at a low to moderate baseline CPU performance, but sometimes need to burst to significantly higher CPU performance when the demand rises. These workloads don’t require the use of the full CPU all the time, but occasionally will need to burst to finish some tasks more quickly. Many applications such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, small databases, micro services, servers for proof-of-concepts, build servers, and code repositories fit into this model.

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Av2 Standard

Av2 Standard is the latest generation of A-series virtual machines with similar CPU performance and faster disk. These virtual machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases. Like the prior A Standard generation, Av2 virtual machines will include load balancing and auto-scaling at no additional charge.

D1-4/DS 1-4

The D1-4/DS 1-4 instances involve provision of powerful CPU, memory and local disk combinations for most production applications.

For persistent storage, please use variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

D1-5 v2

The Dv2-series virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors with the Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0. The D1-5 v2 sizes offer a balanced combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk for most production workloads.

The Dsv2-series virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors with the Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0 and support Premium SSDs disk storage. The Dsv2-series sizes offer a balanced combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk for most production workloads. The pricing and billing meters for Dsv2-series virtual machines are the same as Dv2-series. See pricing for disks .

D2 – D64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The D v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The D v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Ds v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

The Ds v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The Ds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. D2d – D64d v4 (latest generation)

The Dd v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would benefit from these new VM series.

The Dd v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, select Dds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

D2ds – D64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Dds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

The Dds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

Computing optimization: high CPU to memory. Suitable for medium-traffic Web server, network device, batch processing and App Services

Fs v2 Series

The Fsv2-series virtual machines provide 2-GiB of RAM and 8 GB of local temporary storage (SSD) per vCPU(s) and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The Fsv2-series VMs are hyper-threaded and based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), or the Intel Xeon® Platinum 8168 (Skylake) processor. These virtual machines are ideal for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming. The Fsv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

F Series

The F-series virtual machines feature 2-GiB RAM and 16 GiB of local SSD temporary storage per CPU core and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The F-series sizes are based Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processor. These virtual machines are suitable for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSDs disks, use the Fs-series VMs. The pricing and billing meters for Fs sizes are the same as F-series. See pricing for disks .

Memory prioritization: high memory to core ratio. Suitable for relational database servers, medium- and large-scale caches and memory analysis.

E2 – E64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The E v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Es v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2s – E64s v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The Es v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2d – E64d v4 (latest generation)

The Ed v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB.) These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications and applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would also benefit from these new VM series.

You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD disk storage, select Eds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

E2ds – E64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Eds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Edv4/Edsv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM, fast and large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

For persistent storage, please use variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

D11-15 v2 virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The D11-15 v2 VMs are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The D15i v2 instance is isolated to hardware and dedicated to a single customer.

M-series

M-series virtual machines are hyper-threaded and based on either the Intel® Xeon® E7-8890 v3 2.5GHz (Haswell) or the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8280M (Cascade Lake). The M-series provides up to 128 cores and 4.0 TiB of memory, providing unparalleled computational performance to support large in-memory workloads.

All the M-series virtual machine sizes will have the ability to use both standard and premium persistent disks. The pricing and billing meters for Ms sizes are the same as M-series.

M-series VMs are certified by SAP for SAP HANA OLTP and OLAP workloads. For more information on running SAP HANA on Azure, please refer to the SAP solutions .

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Constrained vCPUs capable

These new VM sizes are optimized for database workloads like SQL Server that often require high memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth, but not a high CPU core count. They constrain the VM vCPU(s) count to reduce the cost of software licensing, all while maintaining the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth. The licensing charged for SQL Server will be constrained to the active vCPU(s) count while the compute cost, which includes OS licensing, remains the same one as the original size based on “underlying vCPU(s)”. These new VM sizes allow customer workloads to use the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth while optimizing their software licensing cost.

Category:
  • All
  • General Purpose
  • Computing Prioritization
  • Memory Prioritization

The following prices include the pricing of both Machine Learning Virtual Machine compute and SQL Server license.

General purpose: balancing of CPU to memory ratio. Suitable for testing and development of small- and medium-sized databases and low- and medium-traffic Web servers.

A0-4 – Basic

A Basic is an economical option for development workloads, test servers, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases.

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

A Series Standard

The A Series standard tier supports applications requiring load balancing and autoscaling. These Virtual Machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repository, low-traffic websites and Web Apps, microservices, early product trials and small databases.

B Series

B series are economical virtual machines that provide a low-cost option for workloads which typically run at a low to moderate baseline CPU performance, but sometimes need to burst to significantly higher CPU performance when the demand rises. These workloads don’t require the use of the full CPU all the time, but occasionally will need to burst to finish some tasks more quickly. Many applications such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, small databases, micro services, servers for proof-of-concepts, build servers, and code repositories fit into this model.

*The following prices are tax-inclusive.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Av2 Standard

Av2 Standard is the latest generation of A-series virtual machines with similar CPU performance and faster disk. These virtual machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases. Like the prior A Standard generation, Av2 virtual machines will include load balancing and auto-scaling at no additional charge.

D1-4/DS 1-4

The D1-4/DS 1-4 instances involve provision of powerful CPU, memory and local disk combinations for most production applications.

For persistent storage, please use variable “DS” Virtual Machine, and purchase Premium Storage separately. The pricing and billing meters of the DS size are the same as the D Series.

D2 – D64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The D v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The D v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Ds v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2d – D64d v4 (latest generation)

The Dd v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would benefit from these new VM series.

The Dd v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, select Dds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

D2ds – D64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Dds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

The Dds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

Computing optimization: high CPU to memory. Suitable for medium-traffic Web server, network device, batch processing and App Services

F Series

The F-series virtual machines feature 2-GiB RAM and 16 GiB of local SSD temporary storage per CPU core and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The F-series sizes are based Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processor. These virtual machines are suitable for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming.

Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. To use Premium SSDs disks, use the Fs-series VMs. The pricing and billing meters for Fs sizes are the same as F-series. See pricing for disks .

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

E2 – E64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The E v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Es v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D11-14 v2 virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. The D11-14 v2 VMs are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The D15i v2 instance is isolated to hardware and dedicated to a single customer.

D11-15S v2 virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), the Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1 GHz (Skylake), the 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v4 (Broadwell) or the 2.4 GHz Intel Xeon® E5-2673 v3 (Haswell) processors. Constrained vCPUs capable

These new VM sizes are optimized for database workloads like SQL Server that often require high memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth, but not a high CPU core count. They constrain the VM vCPU(s) count to reduce the cost of software licensing, all while maintaining the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth. The licensing charged for SQL Server will be constrained to the active vCPU(s) count while the compute cost, which includes OS licensing, remains the same one as the original size based on “underlying vCPU(s)”. These new VM sizes allow customer workloads to use the same memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth while optimizing their software licensing cost.

  • General Purpose
  • Computing Prioritization
  • Memory Prioritization
  • GPU

General purpose: balancing of CPU to memory ratio. Suitable for testing and development of small- and medium-sized databases and low- and medium-traffic Web servers.

A0-4 – Basic

A Basic is an economical option for development workloads, test servers, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

B Series

B series are economical virtual machines that provide a low-cost option for workloads which typically run at a low to moderate baseline CPU performance, but sometimes need to burst to significantly higher CPU performance when the demand rises. These workloads don’t require the use of the full CPU all the time, but occasionally will need to burst to finish some tasks more quickly. Many applications such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, small databases, micro services, servers for proof-of-concepts, build servers, and code repositories fit into this model.

*Monthly pricing estimates are based on 744 hours of usage per month.

Av2 Standard

Av2 Standard is the latest generation of A-series virtual machines with similar CPU performance and faster disk. These virtual machines are suitable for development workloads, build servers, code repositories, low-traffic websites and web applications, micro services, early product experiments, and small databases. Like the prior A Standard generation, Av2 virtual machines will include load balancing and auto-scaling at no additional charge.

The Dv3-series virtual machines run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors in a hyper-threaded configuration. The Dv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and local disk well suited for most production workloads.

Dsv3

The Dsv3-series support premium storage and run on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), Intel® Xeon® 8171M 2.1GHz (Skylake), Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v4 2.3 GHz (Broadwell), or the Intel® Xeon® E5-2673 v3 2.4 GHz (Haswell) processors with Intel Turbo Boost Technology 2.0 and feature Intel® Hyper-Threading Technology. The Dsv3-series sizes offer a combination of vCPU(s), memory, and temporary storage well suited for most production workloads.

The VM pricing and billing meters for Dsv3-series are the same as Dv3-series. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2 – D64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The D v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The D v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Ds v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

The Ds v4 series virtual machines feature the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Dv4/Dsv4 virtual machine sizes do not have any temporary storage. The Ds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs and memory able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

D2d – D64d v4 (latest generation)

The Dd v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage. For example, applications that need large numbers of reads and writes to temporary storage or applications that need temporary storage data for scratch files or caches would benefit from these new VM series.

The Dd v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. To use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, select Dds v4 VMs. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

D2ds – D64ds v4 (latest generation)

The Dds v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL processor. This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ddv4/Ddsv4 virtual machine sizes feature fast, large local SSD storage (up to 2,400 GiB) and are well suited for applications that benefit from low latency, high-speed local storage, such as applications that need fast reads and writes to temporary storage, or applications that need temporary storage for caches or temporary files.

The Dds v4 VM sizes offer a combination of vCPUs, memory, and temporary storage able to meet the requirements associated with most production workloads. You can attach Standard SSDs, Standard HDDs, and Premium SSDs disk storage to these VMs. You can also attach Ultra Disk storage based on its regional availability. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines.

Fs v2 Series new

The Fsv2-series virtual machines provide 2-GiB of RAM and 8 GB of local temporary storage (SSD) per vCPU(s) and are optimized for compute intensive workloads. The Fsv2-series VMs are hyper-threaded and based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors), or the Intel Xeon® Platinum 8168 (Skylake) processor. These virtual machines are ideal for scenarios like batch processing, web servers, analytics, and gaming. The Fsv2 VMs can be attached to Premium SSD or Ultra Disk persistent storage. Persistent storage disks are billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .

E2 – E64 v4 (latest generation without temporary storage)

The E v4 series virtual machines are based on the Intel® Xeon® Platinum 8272CL (second generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processor.) This custom processor runs at a base speed of 2.5Ghz and can achieve up to 3.4Ghz all core turbo frequency. The Ev4/Esv4 virtual machine sizes feature up to 504 GiB of RAM and do not have any temporary storage. These virtual machines are ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications. You can attach Standard SSDs, and Standard HDDs disk storage to these VMs. If you prefer to use Premium SSD or Ultra Disk storage, please select the Es v4 virtual machines instead. Disk storage is billed separately from virtual machines. See pricing for disks .
